title: ranunculus pumilio var. pilulifer
intro: Ranunculus pumilio var. pilulifer, also known as the small-flowered creeping buttercup, is a flowering plant species that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant contains ranunculin, a toxic compound, but it also has medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus pumilio var. pilulifer include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and prevent infection. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxic compounds.
